topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The knock on the door was sharp, a jarring intrusion into the quiet monotony of my afternoon. I peered through the peephole, a muscle ticking in my jaw. It was a package, undeniably. The delivery notice confirmed it was addressed to the former occupant, Mrs. Henderson. A faint, sour taste rose in my throat. I didn't move immediately, just let the silence stretch, a deliberate, slow assessment of the situation. When I finally opened the door, the cardboard box looked flimsy, cheap. I took it, the paper crinkling under my grip like dry leaves. My eyes scanned the address label, a flicker of something cold crossing my features before I tucked the item into the corner of the hall closet, far from my view.

[story 2]

I watched the delivery driver leave, a smudge of grime on his uniform. The package sat on my doorstep, an unwelcome punctuation mark on the end of a tenancy. It was clearly meant for someone who had long since vacated this unit. A slow, deliberate sigh escaped my lips, a sound laced with profound weariness. I didn't bother examining the contents; the very notion felt beneath my notice. My gaze drifted over the street, dismissing the whole affair as utterly irrelevant. I nudged the box with the toe of my shoe, a slight, almost imperceptible movement of dismissal, before stepping around it as if it were a piece of unpleasant debris.

[story 3]

When the mail slot rattled, I paused mid-sip of my lukewarm tea. Another delivery. I retrieved the parcel, the weight of it feeling disproportionate to its size. I unfolded the flimsy wrapping, my expression hardening into something unyielding. Reading the name—the name of the person who had occupied this space before me—sent a distinct ripple of distaste through me. I didn't open it. Instead, I held it loosely, turning it over in my hands as if examining something soiled. My shoulders tightened, a subtle clenching that spoke volumes about my disapproval of the intrusion. With a curt nod, I placed it on the nearest shelf, a clear signal that this unwelcome object held no significance for me.
